brief_document.title: Workflow start lifecycle transition
brief_document.purpose: Implement the start of a workflow's life: assign its identifiers, create its single-writer Recorder, record WorkflowStarted through it, spawn its process under the right supervisor, register the handle (with its completion notifier), and return it. This is the entry point start_workflow drives.
brief_document.task: Implement lifecycle/start.rs: a start operation that takes a loaded workflow type and an input Payload, assigns a WorkflowId and RunId, creates the workflow's AD Recorder (AD-002) — the single writer for its history — appends WorkflowStarted through that Recorder (the Recorder owns the expected-sequence head; the engine never appends to the store directly, per AD CO6), resolves the deployed entry module/function via the loader record, spawns the workflow process under its per-type supervisor with trap-exit set, registers the WorkflowHandle (including a completion notifier and its Recorder), and returns it. Scope is start only — cancel/complete/fail are AE-009 and suspend/resume are AE-010.

requirements.title: Assign identifiers and append WorkflowStarted through the Recorder
requirements.spec: WHEN starting a workflow, THE SYSTEM SHALL assign a fresh WorkflowId and RunId, create the workflow's single-writer Recorder (AD-002), and append WorkflowStarted (workflow type, input Payload, timestamp) through that Recorder, which owns the expected-sequence head for the new history.
acceptance: start.rs assigns a new WorkflowId and RunId for the execution.

It creates the AD Recorder for the new workflow (initial sequence head 0) and appends WorkflowStarted (workflow type + input Payload) through it; the engine does not call EventStore::append directly and does not compute expected_seq itself — the Recorder is the single writer and the sequence-head authority (AD CO6/CO7).
A Recorder/append error surfaces as EngineError::Store (or EngineError::Durability) with no process spawned.
A test starts a workflow against InMemoryStore and asserts the first event in history is WorkflowStarted at sequence 0 with the given type and input.

requirements.title: Resolve deployed entry module and spawn under the supervisor
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the workflow type to its deployed entry module and function via the loader record and SHALL spawn the workflow process under its per-type supervisor with trap-exit set. IF the workflow type is not loaded, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::WorkflowNotFound and append nothing.
acceptance: start.rs looks up the LoadedWorkflow record (from AE-005) to get the deployed entry module/function and version.
It ensures the per-type supervisor (AE-006) and spawns the workflow process under it via RuntimeHandle::spawn_workflow, with trap-exit set per the supervision policy (AE-006).
An unloaded workflow type returns EngineError::WorkflowNotFound and produces no WorkflowStarted append (the type check precedes the append).
A test starts an unknown type and asserts WorkflowNotFound with an empty store.

requirements.title: Register the handle (with completion notifier) and return it
requirements.spec: WHEN the workflow process is spawned, THE SYSTEM SHALL register a WorkflowHandle keyed by (WorkflowId, RunId) with status Running and a freshly-created completion notifier, and SHALL return the handle to the caller.
acceptance: On successful spawn, a WorkflowHandle (pid, type, version, status Running, residency Resident, the workflow's Recorder, and a completion notifier) is inserted into the registry under (WorkflowId, RunId).

The completion notifier is created here at start and stored on the handle; it is the channel a later result() awaiter blocks on and that the terminal transitions (AE-009) fulfil. A oneshot is insufficient (multiple awaiters); use a multi-consumer notifier (e.g. a watch/broadcast of the eventual TerminalOutcome) so any number of result() callers can observe completion.
start returns the WorkflowHandle to the caller.
A test starts a workflow and asserts the registry contains a Running, Resident handle with a completion notifier for the new (WorkflowId, RunId) and that the returned handle matches.
IF the registry lock is poisoned, the operation returns EngineError::RegistryPoisoned.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement cancel/complete/fail — those are AE-009.
SHALL NOT implement suspend/resume — those are AE-010.
SHALL NOT implement the replay path used to re-create handles (and Recorders) on restart — that is AD (recovery, AD-009).
SHALL NOT define the Engine public method — start_workflow on Engine is the engine surface; this brief provides the lifecycle operation it calls.
SHALL NOT append to the EventStore directly — all appends go through the Recorder (AD CO6).
verification: cargo test -p aion passes against InMemoryStore.
WorkflowStarted is appended before the handle is registered; an unloaded type appends nothing.

checklist.text: start_workflow assigns a WorkflowId and RunId, appends WorkflowStarted via the store, spawns the workflow process for the loaded type, registers the handle, and returns a WorkflowHandle.

stories.text: As a workflow caller, I want to start a workflow by type and get a handle so that I can later await its result or cancel it by ID.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
